blurb: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire published a point-by-point rebuttal to Open USD, the fee-free stablecoin launched June 30 by a 140-company consortium including Visa, Stripe, Coinbase and BlackRock, arguing USDC's distribution, liquidity, and multi-year regulatory licensing in the EU and Japan cannot be easily replicated. The defense followed a slide in Circle's stock, with Open USD set to become the default stablecoin for businesses on Stripe.

title: Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
blurb: President Trump and his family earned an estimated windfall from the World Liberty Financial memecoin, which generated large losses for the hundreds of thousands of retail investors who bought in. The venture underscores the concentration of gains among insiders in celebrity token launches, where founders and early holders capture value while later buyers absorb the drawdown.

title: The SEC Doesn’t Want to Be in the Biz of Picking ETF Winners
blurb: The SEC is reconsidering its ETF oversight framework, aiming to step back from a gatekeeping role that has effectively let regulators decide which products reach market. A more permissive, rules-based approach would lower the approval bar for crypto ETFs, shortening the path for spot funds tied to assets beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum.

title: Phantom Hires Ventuals Founders After Hyperliquid Perps Venue Winds Down
blurb: Phantom, the largest Solana wallet by market share, hired the three-person team behind Ventuals, the Hyperliquid-based pre-IPO perpetuals venue that wound down in mid-June after more than $650M in lifetime volume. The acqui-hire, which excludes Ventuals' product, deepens Phantom's derivatives push after it added Hyperliquid-powered in-wallet perps in July 2025 and expanded into equity and pre-IPO markets.

title: Magic Eden, Founders Sued by $ME Buyers Over Broken 'Utility' Promises
blurb: Three $ME buyers sued Magic Eden and its four co-founders in federal court in New York, alleging the company promoted use cases like multichain trading, governance, and staking rewards, then delayed or abandoned them. The class action brings New York consumer-protection claims rather than securities claims, testing whether a token's marketed utility can support recovery for holders; $ME trades near $0.056, down roughly 99% from its high.

title: Pump.fun's PUMP Buybacks Top $400M as Token Stays Flat
blurb: Pump.fun has repurchased and burned over $400M of its PUMP token across 346 days, removing roughly 145.5 billion tokens from circulation under the 50%-of-revenue buyback policy it adopted in April. The burns have yet to lift the price, with PUMP down about 16% over the past 30 days and trading roughly 83% below its September record as memecoin fee generation cools to $23.5M monthly.

title: Drift Protocol Rebrands to Velocity DEX Ahead of Relaunch
blurb: Solana perpetuals exchange Drift Protocol rebranded to Velocity DEX ahead of a relaunch as a leaner, perpetuals-only venue settled in USDT, with a private beta going to select partners in the coming days. The rebrand follows an April 1 exploit that drained roughly $295.4 million from Drift's vaults, which Mandiant attributed to a DPRK-linked actor and which the protocol is repaying through a token-based recovery framework backed by a $127.5 million Tether commitment.

title: Jefferies warns against buying the dip in Circle as Open USD raises new competition fears
blurb: Jefferies advised against buying the dip in Circle (CRCL), which bounced 5% Wednesday after a 17% plunge, arguing that competition from the Stripe-, Coinbase-, and BlackRock-backed Open USD consortium could pressure USDC's supply growth and market share. Circle holds roughly 25% of the $300B stablecoin market and derives about 95% of revenue from reserve interest, leaving it exposed as banks and fintechs launch rival tokens.

title: Avalanche Treasury Corp Stock Crashes 93%, Warns SEC It May Not Survive the Year
blurb: Avalanche Treasury Corp, the largest publicly traded AVAX holder, warned the SEC of substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern after its Nasdaq-listed stock fell 93% in a month to below $0.73. The company paid roughly $265M for AVAX now worth about $123M, has pledged 7.8M of its 13.8M tokens as loan collateral, and holds a market cap under $30M.

title: Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400 million crypto Ponzi case
blurb: Former Goliath Ventures CEO Christopher Delgado pleaded guilty to wire fraud and money laundering over a crypto Ponzi scheme that solicited investors with promised monthly returns of 3% to 8% from claimed liquidity pools, causing at least $250 million in losses. He agreed to forfeit 8 properties, 11 vehicles, and other luxury assets, and faces sentencing on October 8.

title: Ethereum Institutional Launches as Independent Nonprofit to Court Banks and Asset Managers
blurb: Ethereum Institutional launched July 1 as a standalone nonprofit, spinning out the Ethereum Foundation's institutional go-to-market team into an independently funded entity aimed at banks and asset managers. Led by executive director David Walsh and SharpLink CEO Joseph Chalom, with BitMine's Tom Lee on the board, the group claims 500+ institutional relationships and a forum representing roughly $250T in combined assets under management.

title: Bitcoin breaks above $60,000 after Fed Chair Warsh said inflation risks has come down
blurb: Bitcoin retook $60,000, climbing more than 2% over the past 24 hours after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks have come down while reaffirming the central bank's 2% target. Warsh declined to guide on the next rate decision but flagged that AI-driven capex could expand the economy's supply side, with what he called major implications for future monetary policy.

title: Securitize Shareholders Approve Merger, Paving Way for First Publicly Traded Tokenization Company
blurb: Securitize Corp. begins trading on the NYSE July 2 under ticker SECZ after Cantor Equity Partners II shareholders approved the SPAC merger, making it the first publicly traded tokenization company in the US. The deal delivers roughly $400M in gross proceeds, including a $225M PIPE, giving the BlackRock BUIDL issuance platform a public balance sheet to expand regulated digital-securities infrastructure across the US and EU.

title: OKX and Coinbase Race to Sign Up Binance's Displaced EU Users Before MiCA Deadline
blurb: OKX and Coinbase launched competing deposit-bonus campaigns to capture European users losing access to Binance, which restricts EEA services on July 1 after withdrawing its MiCA license application in Greece. OKX, licensed in Malta, is offering up to 8% on transfers, while Coinbase is targeting Coinbase One subscribers across seven markets, both exploiting MiCA's licensing requirement as a structural moat against unauthorized rivals.

title: BNY Adds USDC to Institutional Custody Platform in Expanded Circle Partnership
blurb: BNY, the world's largest custodian with $59.4T in assets under custody, made USDC the first stablecoin supported on its Digital Asset Custody platform, letting institutional clients hold, transfer, mint, and redeem Circle's token alongside traditional assets. The integration, enabled by the GENIUS Act's custody authority for chartered banks, collapses the handoff between traditional custodian and stablecoin-infra provider into one operational framework, with BNY planning to add more issuers over time.

title: BlackRock Adds Ethena's Synthetic Dollar to Its $20T Aladdin Risk Management Platform
blurb: BlackRock will list Ethena's USDe synthetic dollar as an approved digital asset on Aladdin, its risk platform managing over $20T for banks, insurers, and pension funds, while BUIDL becomes the primary reserve for Ethena's whitelabel stablecoins. The tie-up is the deepest link yet between the largest asset manager and a DeFi derivatives protocol, signaling traditional finance is moving capital onchain rather than building rival rails.

title: Strategy Authorizes up to $1.25B of Bitcoin Sales as Saylor Formalizes Capital Pivot
blurb: Strategy's board approved a Digital Credit Capital Framework authorizing up to $1.25B in Bitcoin sales to fund dividends, interest, and buybacks, formalizing that the largest corporate BTC holder may now sell from its 847,363 BTC stack. The move codifies a capital pivot for a company built on never selling, arriving as its STRC preferred stock trades below target and its Bitcoin position sits roughly $14B underwater.

title: Robinhood rolls out public blockchain as it expands deeper into crypto
blurb: Robinhood launched the public mainnet for Robinhood Chain, an Arbitrum-based Layer 2 that brings tokenized stock trading live in more than 120 countries and adds Robinhood Earn, a self-custody lending product paying an estimated 7% yield on its USDG stablecoin. The move pushes Robinhood past its brokerage roots into onchain infrastructure, letting users trade tokenized equities around the clock and post them as DeFi collateral.

title: Robinhood Rolls Out Perpetual Futures Tied to Commodities, ETFs
blurb: Robinhood launched perpetual futures tied to commodities, ETFs, and currencies for European users, extending the round-the-clock trading model that originated in crypto markets to traditional asset classes. The move deepens Robinhood's European derivatives push and lets retail traders hold leveraged, expiry-free positions on assets that normally trade only during exchange hours.

title: Open Standard Unveils Open USD, a Bank- and Tech-Backed Stablecoin Governed by Its Users
blurb: A consortium of more than 140 companies, including Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, BlackRock, BNY and Coinbase, introduced Open USD (OUSD), a dollar stablecoin that returns nearly all reserve earnings to the businesses that adopt it rather than to a single issuer. Governed collectively through Open Standard and launching natively on Solana in 2026, OUSD inverts the incumbent float-income model, though its reserve custodians, attestation cadence and issuing entity remain undisclosed.

title: Visa, Stripe and 140 others back new Open USD stablecoin to challenge Tether
blurb: A consortium of more than 140 companies, including Visa, Stripe, BlackRock, Mastercard, and Coinbase, is backing Open USD (OUSD), a new stablecoin from Open Standard LLC launching later this year to challenge Tether and Circle. The token lets businesses mint and redeem with no fees or volume limits and returns most reserve revenue to partners, breaking from the issuer-keeps-the-interest model that made USDT and USDC dominant.

title: Robinhood Launches Robinhood Chain Mainnet, Adds Stock Tokens, Onchain Lending, and Agentic Crypto Trading
blurb: Robinhood launched the public mainnet of Robinhood Chain, its Arbitrum-based Layer 2 for tokenized real-world assets, alongside Stock Tokens, onchain lending, and agentic crypto trading. Stock Tokens are tokenized debt securities issued by Robinhood Assets (Jersey) Limited that track equity prices without conferring ownership, tradable around the clock in 120-plus countries via Uniswap, 1inch, and other DEXs, and usable as DeFi collateral.

title: Supreme Court Overturns Humphrey's Executor, Clearing Trump to Fire SEC and CFTC Commissioners
blurb: The Supreme Court overturned Humphrey's Executor in a 6-3 ruling in Trump v. Slaughter, letting the president fire commissioners at independent agencies without cause and extending removal power over roughly two dozen bodies, including the SEC and CFTC. The decision lands as the Senate weighs an August floor vote on the CLARITY Act, giving Trump leverage to remove any Democratic commissioner he might appoint to secure the crypto-oversight bill's passage.

title: Crypto firms have spent $189M so far on 2026 US election, report says
blurb: Crypto firms have poured $189M into 2026 US election campaigns so far, according to a new report, already exceeding much of their 2024 midterm spending. The push reflects a coordinated bet on friendlier regulation from Congress and agencies like the SEC and CFTC, as stablecoin and market-structure bills remain unresolved on Capitol Hill.

title: JPMorgan's Kinexys Blockchain Hits $4 Trillion, Adds Five APAC Currencies
blurb: JPMorgan's Kinexys blockchain crossed $4 trillion in cumulative transactions and added the Australian dollar, Hong Kong dollar, Japanese yen, Chinese renminbi, and Singapore dollar to its permissioned Blockchain Deposit Account network, extending 24/7 settlement to APAC trade corridors. The expansion, with average daily volume above $7 billion, pushes JPMorgan's bank-rail tokenized deposits ahead of public-chain stablecoins for regulated institutions that want near-instant settlement without new counterparty or KYC risk.

title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
blurb: Cloudflare announced its Monetization Gateway, an engine that lets customers charge for any Cloudflare-protected resource including web pages, datasets, APIs, and MCP tools, with payments settling in stablecoins over the x402 protocol. The gateway handles payment verification and enforcement at the edge, giving operators a single control plane for usage-based pricing as AI agents become the dominant consumers of web content.

title: New York Life Partners with Centrifuge on Tokenized Corporate Bonds
blurb: New York Life Investment Management, an $807B asset manager, partnered with tokenization platform Centrifuge to launch HYB, its first tokenized fund and one of the first high-yield corporate bond strategies onchain. Subscriptions and redemptions settle in USDC, and the fund targets stablecoin issuers, DeFi users, and DAO treasuries seeking yield beyond the Treasury-backed products that dominate tokenized real-world assets to date.

title: Cloudflare Launches Monetization Gateway for Stablecoin Payments via x402
blurb: Cloudflare opened a waitlist for its Monetization Gateway, a tool that lets operators charge for any page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind its network, with payments settling in stablecoins over the x402 protocol. The product targets sub-cent, per-request billing for AI agents rather than human visitors, and follows AWS wiring Coinbase's x402 into CloudFront in June to let publishers charge agents per request in USDC.

title: DraftKings Launches Proprietary Prediction Markets Exchange With $3.4B Annualized Consumer Volume
blurb: DraftKings launched DKeX, a proprietary prediction markets exchange built on the CFTC license it acquired through Railbird, moving off third-party infrastructure to own the full stack behind its event-contracts business. The platform reported roughly $3.4B in annualized consumer volume and $11.3B in total trading volume for the week ended June 21, and its 30-state sportsbook footprint gives it immediate distribution against Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: Visa, Mastercard and Coinbase have launched a new global stablecoin
blurb: A consortium of over 140 businesses led by Visa, Mastercard, and Coinbase launched Open Standard, a stablecoin network whose dollar-pegged coin, Open USD, ships later this year under the GENIUS Act's 1:1 reserve rules. The move puts the card networks directly into stablecoin issuance, though research still shows stablecoins are used mostly for crypto trading rather than everyday purchases.

title: Restaurants can now accept orders placed directly from ChatGPT and Claude thanks to Square's new, low-fee, no setup integration
blurb: Square launched a ChatGPT app and Claude plugin that let restaurants accept orders placed by consumers and their AI agents directly inside those platforms, pulling live catalog, pricing, and stock from Square with no technical setup required. Square waives the marketplace commissions that DoorDash and Uber Eats charge up to 30%, billing only its standard online-ordering fee of 2.9% to 3.3% plus $0.30 per transaction.

title: SEC Opens 60-Day Comment Period on 'Novel' ETF Rules as Prediction Market Funds Pile Up
blurb: The SEC opened a 60-day comment period on how it regulates "Novel ETFs," a category spanning crypto-asset funds and prediction-market products, publishing release 33-11426 as event-contract ETF applications stack up in its queue. The review lands amid surging prediction-market volume, with Kalshi closing a $1B raise at a $22B valuation in May before reportedly seeking a $40B mark, signaling the SEC wants a standardized path before clearing the backlog.

title: Chinese Exile Miles Guo Sentenced to 30 Years for $1B Crypto Fraud Scheme
blurb: A federal court in the Southern District of New York sentenced exiled Chinese businessman Miles Guo to 30 years in prison and ordered him to forfeit $889 million for a $1B fraud scheme that included a fake cryptocurrency called Himalaya Coin. Prosecutors said the "H Coin" and "H Dollar" tokens were internal spreadsheet entries rather than blockchain assets, underscoring how unregistered crypto offerings remain a primary vehicle for securities fraud enforcement.

title: CFTC Expands Polymarket Probe to Staged Trades and Fake Wins
blurb: The CFTC expanded its Polymarket investigation to cover staged trades and fabricated winning bets, Bloomberg reported, widening a probe that began as an advertising-disclosure inquiry into the platform's paid-influencer scheme. The escalation follows a bipartisan Senate letter citing a WSJ finding that paid creators filmed roughly $900,000 in fake wins on dummy sites, and it revives jurisdictional questions about whether the CFTC can police event-contract platforms as a gaming regulator.

title: Binance and Anchorage Digital Launch Off-Exchange Settlement for Institutional Traders
blurb: Binance and Anchorage Digital launched an off-exchange settlement integration on Anchorage's Atlas infrastructure, letting institutional traders tap Binance's liquidity while keeping assets in the custody of the first federally chartered US crypto bank rather than on the exchange. Institutions can pledge crypto, cash equivalents, and tokenized real-world assets including BlackRock's BUIDL, Circle's USYC, and Franklin Templeton's iBENJI as collateral, separating custody from execution the way traditional finance does.

title: SumUp launches consumer account paying users to shop small
blurb: SumUp launched a consumer bank account offering 5% cashback on purchases within its merchant network, extending the small-business payments company into personal banking. The two-sided play waives transaction fees for merchants accepting SumUp consumer users, pitting SumUp against Revolut, Monzo, and N26 in a crowded European neobank market that Wise and Klarna have also entered.

title: Alibaba, Payment Firm Will Pay $600 Million to Resolve US Probe
blurb: Alibaba and its US payment processor agreed to pay $600 million to settle a Justice Department investigation into whether they failed to prevent the sale and importation of illegal pharmaceuticals and controlled substances. The settlement signals that DOJ is willing to hold payment processors accountable for illicit-goods transactions flowing across their rails, raising the compliance bar for any platform handling high-risk merchant categories.

title: Scaling Network Analysis for Fraud Prevention with BigQuery Graph
blurb: Curve, the UK financial super-app, moved its fraud-detection network analysis to BigQuery Graph, using native GQL to trace the multi-hop links between accounts that share devices, funding cards, or contact details. Running graph queries directly inside its existing BigQuery warehouse let Curve surface organized fraud rings without migrating to a dedicated graph database, avoiding costly self-joins and producing significant transaction savings.

title: Finance App Network Maker Plaid Is Said to Consider US IPO
blurb: Plaid is considering a US IPO, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a possible public debut for the company whose network connects fintech apps to customer bank accounts. A listing would test public-market appetite for account-connectivity infrastructure and set a valuation benchmark for the open-banking layer that Stripe, Ramp, and countless neobanks depend on.

title: Morgan Stanley cut its riskiest reconciliation job in half — by making its agents less autonomous
blurb: Morgan Stanley deployed an agentic system called FIXR to handle P&L reconciliation across its Finance, Risk, Operations, and Trade Capture systems, cutting the time to clear daily "breaks" from up to six hours per book to two or three and saving roughly 1,500 controller hours a week. The gains came from keeping humans tightly in the loop, with agents codifying repeated controller decisions into durable rules rather than acting autonomously.

title: Europe's MiCA rollout sparks debate over who wins under new crypto rules
blurb: The European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation is now fully in force, requiring any firm serving customers in the 27-nation bloc to hold a MiCA license or stop operating, with thousands of providers facing suspension as of June 30. One Maltese lawyer estimates the licensed pool could shrink from roughly 3,000 registered CASPs to 300 or 400, favoring larger firms while smaller players relocate to jurisdictions like Dubai.

title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
blurb: Trump reported at least $1.4B in 2025 earnings from crypto and memecoin ventures in his latest annual financial disclosure, making digital assets a material share of the sitting president's personal income. The figure sharpens the conflict-of-interest scrutiny facing his administration's crypto agenda, as the same White House shapes stablecoin rules, SEC enforcement posture, and federal digital-asset policy.

title: Google Must Pay Nearly $2 Billion to Klarna in Antitrust Case
blurb: A Swedish court ordered Google to pay Klarna nearly $2B after ruling the search giant favored its own price-comparison service over Klarna's in search results. The decision adds to Google's mounting antitrust liabilities in Europe and gives fintechs that depend on search distribution a precedent for challenging how platforms rank competing consumer-finance products.

title: The Lowest-Logical-Fare Problem
blurb: Expense management software can automate a travel policy but not the judgment behind it, because the cheapest bookable option often isn't the cheapest actual outcome once you count connections, drive times, and a traveler's wasted hours. The author calls the real target the "lowest logical fare," a per-trip judgment call that no hard-coded rule survives. The moment you write the rule down, reality produces the exception.

title: Alibaba and its US payment processor AUS agree to pay $600M to resolve DOJ allegations that they failed to prevent illegal sales of drugs and other products (Reuters)
blurb: Alibaba and its US payment processor AUS agreed to pay $600M to settle DOJ allegations that they failed to prevent illegal sales of drugs and other products through their platforms. The settlement puts payment processors on notice that facilitating transactions for illicit goods carries direct liability, raising the compliance burden on marketplace payment operators handling high-risk merchant flows.

title: The SEC Doesn’t Want to Be in the Biz of Picking ETF Winners
blurb: The SEC is reconsidering its approach to ETF oversight, moving away from a case-by-case approval model that effectively let it decide which products reach the market. A more standardized framework would lower the barrier for issuers to launch new ETFs, shifting winner-picking from the regulator to investors and speeding time-to-market across the industry.

title: Circle CEO Rebuts OUSD Pitch, Defends USDC's Network Effects After Stock Slide
blurb: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire published a point-by-point rebuttal on July 1 to OUSD, the fee-free stablecoin launched by the Stripe-backed Open Standard consortium, arguing that USDC's distribution, liquidity depth, and regulatory licensing in the EU and Japan cannot be quickly replicated. The 140-partner OUSD lineup includes Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, and Coinbase but not Circle, Tether, or PayPal, and the rebuttal followed a slide in Circle's stock.

title: Bitcoin breaks above $60,000 after Fed Chair Warsh said inflation risks has come down
blurb: Bitcoin climbed back above $60,000, up more than 2% in the past 24 hours, after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks have come down while reaffirming the central bank's 2% target. Warsh declined to signal the Fed's next rate move and pointed to AI-driven capital spending as a force that could expand the economy's supply side, with potentially large implications for future monetary policy.

title: Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400 million crypto Ponzi case
blurb: Christopher Alexander Delgado, former CEO of Goliath Ventures, pleaded guilty to wire fraud and money laundering in a $400 million crypto Ponzi scheme that promised investors guaranteed monthly returns of 3% to 8% from purported liquidity pools. Investors have since sued JPMorgan for processing roughly $253 million in Goliath-linked deposits despite red flags, putting compliance scrutiny back on the sponsor banks that move fraud proceeds.

title: In Treasury Management, to the Victor go the Spoils
blurb: Legacy banks face a worsening retention problem with commercial clients as a younger generation of CFOs, comfortable with modern software, demands real-time visibility, instant issuance, and clean reconciliation from their treasury management providers. The author argues that tech firms have raised the baseline on these features, and banks that leaned on decades-old cores now must match digital-first competitors or watch business deposits move elsewhere.

title: Jefferies warns against buying the dip in Circle as Open USD raises new competition fears
blurb: Jefferies advised clients against buying the dip in Circle (CRCL), warning that the newly launched Open USD consortium and a wave of bank- and fintech-issued stablecoins threaten USDC's supply growth and roughly 25% share of the $300B stablecoin market. The pressure is structural: Open USD's 140-plus backers, including Stripe, Coinbase, Visa, Mastercard and BlackRock, plan to share reserve income with participants, undercutting the interest-on-reserves model that generates about 95% of Circle's revenue.

title: Apple Pay now lets you pay using American Express points
blurb: American Express Membership Rewards points can now be redeemed directly inside the Apple Pay checkout flow, letting cardholders apply points to cover all or part of any online or in-app purchase without leaving the payment screen. Apple retains no transaction data from these redemptions, and the native integration removes the separate redemption steps that previously pushed points-paying outside the primary checkout.

title: EquiLibre secures Series A at €438 million valutation to scale AI trading agents handling billions daily
blurb: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based lab applying reinforcement learning to live trading, closed a Series A at a valuation above €438M ($500M), led by Creandum in what is reportedly the firm's largest check to date. Most of the capital will fund one of the region's largest compute clusters, backing agents the company says trade billions of dollars daily and have never posted a losing month.

title: Blackstone Offloads Stakes in Trio of Virginia Data Centers
blurb: Blackstone is selling its stakes in three Northern Virginia data centers for $3.5B, exiting part of a bet it placed less than three years ago. The sale signals Blackstone is taking profits near the top of an AI-driven data-center boom, even as it continues to raise and deploy capital into digital infrastructure elsewhere.

title: Inside Meta’s $900 Million India Gamble
blurb: Meta appointed CRED founder Kunal Shah to help lead WhatsApp globally as it pushes to turn the messaging app into a payments and financial-services platform, with India as the central market. The bet hinges on WhatsApp Pay scaling on India's UPI rails, where Meta has struggled against PhonePe and Google Pay despite years of regulatory-capped user rollouts.

title: Big Bank Calls Unleash Trading Frenzy in US Funding Rate Futures
blurb: Divergent forecasts from top Wall Street banks on July's Treasury bill supply surge have ignited heavy trading in US funding-rate futures.

title: Self-driving scaleup Wayve set to test LSE’s Pisces
blurb: Wayve will sell shares next Wednesday on Pisces, the London Stock Exchange Group's new private-markets platform, becoming the first major UK scaleup to use it via an $85M employee tender offer. The debut is a test of Pisces' bid to keep British tech listing at home after a run of weak IPOs and startups floating abroad, and it hands Wayve investor liquidity without a full public offering.
